aiTitle: 使用生成的測試範例驗證系統

content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>我現在沒東西，你有辦法先用自行產生測試範例幫我驗證嗎?</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-06-22</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
對,要按新機制——而且這直接**推翻我上一則的答案**。我上面說「新 skill 加到 `~/.claude/skills`」,在新架構下那是錯的:那層現在是**投影**,不是正本。

## 證據(實際 junction target,A 級)

我抽查了磁碟,`~/.claude/skills` 本身是實體資料夾,但**裡面每個 skill 都是 junction**,指回 canonical:

| Skill | `~/.claude/skills` junction → |
|-------|------|
| `skill-creator` | `G:\AI\AI-canonical\skills\general\skill-creator` |
| `writing-skills` | `G:\AI\AI-canonical\skills\general\writing-skills` |
| `ms-portable-skill-authoring` | `G:\AI\AI-canonical\skills\general\...` |
| `uk-slot-extrabet` | `G:\AI\AI-canonical-corp\skills\slot\uk-slot-extrabet` |
| `uk-slot-state-machine` | `G:\AI\**corp**\skills\slot\...` |

→ 完全吻合 v4 文件的**雙正本**設計:`general` 進個人 repo、`slot`(公司 IP)進 corp repo,再用 `sync.ps1` 投影成 junction。

## 所以新建 skill 的正確流程

**❌ 不要再直接寫 `~/.claude/skills`**(= §9 的「投影 drift / shadow truth」:不會同步到 Kiro,且下次 `sync.ps1` / `bootstrap.ps1` 可能蓋掉或孤立它)。

**✅ 正確順序:**

1. **先決定 domain**(決定進哪個正本 repo):
   - 通用/方法論/工具類 → `G:\AI\AI-canonical\skills\general\<name>\`
   - 綁公司老虎機(slot)→ `G:\AI\AI-canonical-corp\skills\slot\<name>\`
2. **在那個 canonical 路徑下**用 `skill-creator` 產 `SKILL.md`,frontmatter 帶 §4 的 `domain` tag + provenance
3. **跑 `G:\AI\AI-canonical\tools\sync.ps1`** → 自動在 `~/.claude/skills`(和 `~/.kiro/skills`)建 junction,各 CLI 立刻讀得到
4. **半自動鐵則**(§0):進正本前先當提案 → 你一鍵核准 → commit 進 canonical git(留 provenance)

一句話:**寫進 canonical repo 的對應 domain 資料夾,sync 出投影,而不是寫進投影層。**

---

要不要我現在實際幫你跑一遍?你只要告訴我:**這個新 skill 是 `general` 還是 `slot` domain、解決什麼問題**,我就照上面流程建在正確的 canonical 位置並 sync。(建立 / commit 屬寫正本,我會先做成提案給你核准,不直接落地。)

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

- "No output necessary if skipping."

I should wait for actual tool executions and work to be performed in the primary session before generating observations. A user request alone doesn't constitute something to record - I need to see the actual work being done.

I should not output anything at this point since there's no actual work or tool execution to observe yet.

aiTitle: 使用生成的測試範例驗證系統

content:
<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-22T08:09:41.248Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\Cocos_Project\uk_pirates_queen</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\AI-canonical\\\\tools\\\\sync.ps1\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\AI-canonical\\\\tools\\\\sync.ps1\",\"content\":\"#requires -Version 5\\n<#\\n  sync.ps1 — 從 canonical 正本投影 skills 到各 CLI(Windows junction)\\n\\n  來源(兩正本的 skills/<domain>/<name>):\\n    個人 general : $PersonalRoot\\\\skills\\\\general\\\\*\\n    公司 slot    : $CorpRoot\\\\skills\\\\slot\\\\*\\n  目標 CLI:\\n    ~/.kiro/skills, ~/.claude/skills\\n\\n  安全設計:\\n    - 預設 DRY-RUN(只印不動)。加 -Apply 才實際執行。\\n    - 移除既有 junction 用 `rmdir`(只刪連結,不碰正本內容);真實資料夾才用 Remove-Item。\\n    - 冪等:重跑只會重建連結。\\n#>\\nparam([switch]$Apply)\\n\\n$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'\\n$PersonalRoot = 'G:\\\\AI\\\\AI-canonical'\\n$CorpRoot     = 'G:\\\\AI\\\\AI-canonical-corp'\\n$Targets = @(\\n  'C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.kiro\\\\skills',\\n  'C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.claude\\\\skills'\\n)\\n$DryRun = -not $Apply\\n\\nfunction Test-Reparse($path){\\n  $it = Get-Item $path -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue\\n  return ($it -and ($it.Attributes -band [IO.FileAttributes]::ReparsePoint))\\n}\\nfunction Remove-Target($path){\\n  if(-not (Test-Path $path)){ return }\\n  if(Test-Reparse $path){ cmd /c rmdir \\\"$path\\\" | Out-Null }   # junction:只刪連結\\n  else { Remove-Item $path -Recurse -Force }                   # 真實資料夾\\n}\\n\\n# 掃兩個正本,建 skill 名 -> 正本路徑 -ForegroundColor Yellow }\\n      $sources[$_.Name] = $_.FullName\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nWrite-Host (\\\"[mode]    \\\" + $(if($DryRun){'DRY-RUN(只印不動;加 -Apply 才執行)'}else{'APPLY'}))\\nWrite-Host Join-Path $t $name\\n    $exists = Test-Path $dst\\n    $isLink = $exists -and (Test-Reparse $dst)\\n    if($DryRun){\\n      if($isLink){ Write-Host \\\"  [relink]  $name\\\" }\\n      elseif($exists){ Write-Host \\\"  [replace] $name  (現為真實資料夾 -> 改 junction)\\\" }\\n      else { Write-Host \\\"  [link]    $name\\\" }\\n      continue\\n    }\\n    Remove-Target $dst\\n    cmd /c mklink /J \\\"$dst\\\" \\\"$src\\\" | Out-Null\\n    Write-Host \\\"  [ok] $name -> $src\\\"\\n  }\\n}\\n# ─── steering(Kiro only;copy 投影 —— 正本為來源,edit 請改正本再 sync)───\\n$steerSrc = Join-Path $PersonalRoot \\\"steering\\\"\\n$steerDst = \\\"C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.kiro\\\\steering\\\"\\nif(Test-Path $steerSrc){\\n  Write-Host \\\"`n=== steering → $steerDst (copy) ===\\\"\\n  if(-not (Test-Path $steerDst)){ if(-not $DryRun){ New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $steerDst | Out-Null } }\\n  foreach($f in (Get-ChildItem $steerSrc -File -Filter *.md)){\\n    if($DryRun){ Write-Host \\\"  [would-copy] $($f.Name)\\\" }\\n    else { Copy-Item $f.FullName (Join-Path $steerDst $f.Name) -Force; Write-Host \\\"  [copied] $($f.Name)\\\" }\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nif($DryRun){ Write-Host \\\"`n完成(DRY-RUN:沒有任何變更。確認後加 -Apply 執行)。\\\" }\\nelse { Write-Host \\\"`n完成(已套用 junction + steering copy)。\\\" }\\n\",\"numLines\":87,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":87}}"</outcome>

The user is asking me to write a progress summary of the observed session. Let me analyze what happened:

1. **User Request**: The user asked in Chinese "我現在沒東西，你有辦法先用自行產生測試範例幫我驗證嗎?" which means "I don't have anything right now, can you generate test examples yourself to help me verify?"

2. **Claude's Response**: Claude provided a detailed explanation in Chinese about the correct way to create new skills in their system. The key points were:
   - Claude corrected its previous answer about adding skills to `~/.claude/skills`
   - Explained that `~/.claude/skills` is a projection layer (with junctions), not the canonical source
   - Provided evidence from disk inspection showing junction mappings from `~/.claude/skills` to canonical locations in `G:\AI\AI-canonical\skills\` and `G:\AI\AI-canonical-corp\skills\`
   - Explained the v4 architecture with dual canonical repos (personal and corporate)
   - Outlined the correct workflow for creating new skills:
     1. Decide domain (general vs slot)
     2. Create in canonical repo location
     3. Run sync.ps1 to create junctions
     4. Follow approval process before committing
   - Offered to demonstrate the workflow if the user provides domain and problem description

This appears to be about understanding and correcting the architecture for skill creation in a Claude Code environment with a sophisticated canonical/projection setup.

Let me write the summary:

content.text:
<summary>
  <request>Understanding correct skill creation workflow in v4 canonical architecture</request>
  <investigated>Examined actual disk structure of `~/.claude/skills` directory and traced junction targets to canonical repositories. Verified mappings between projection layer (`~/.claude/skills`) and canonical sources (`G:\AI\AI-canonical\skills\general\` and `G:\AI\AI-canonical-corp\skills\slot\`). Reviewed v4 architecture documentation regarding dual canonical repos and sync mechanisms.</investigated>
  <learned>The `~/.claude/skills` directory is a projection layer, not the canonical source. Each skill in this directory is a junction pointing to one of two canonical repositories: `G:\AI\AI-canonical` for general/personal skills, or `G:\AI\AI-canonical-corp` for company IP (slot-related) skills. The correct workflow requires creating skills directly in the appropriate canonical repo, then running `sync.ps1` to generate junctions in the projection layer. Writing directly to `~/.claude/skills` creates drift and risks being overwritten by future sync operations. The v4 architecture enforces a "proposal before commit" rule for canonical repo changes.</learned>
  <completed>Corrected previous incorrect guidance about skill creation location. Documented the complete junction mapping from disk inspection. Defined the 4-step correct workflow: (1) determine domain, (2) create in canonical location, (3) run sync.ps1, (4) follow approval process.</completed>
  <next_steps>Awaiting user's decision on whether to proceed with a practical demonstration of the skill creation workflow. If user provides domain (general vs slot) and problem description, will execute the correct canonical-first creation process with approval step.</next_steps>
  <notes>This represents a significant architectural understanding correction. The session demonstrates the importance of verifying actual system implementation rather than relying on assumptions. The dual-repo canonical architecture (personal + corporate) with junction-based projection is a sophisticated setup designed to maintain provenance and prevent shadow truth scenarios.</notes>
</summary>

The user is reading the sync.ps1 script from the AI-canonical tools directory. This is part of understanding the skill creation workflow in the v4 architecture. The script shows:

1. It syncs skills from two canonical sources (personal and corp repos) to CLI projection layers
2. Uses Windows junctions for the skill directories
3. Has a dry-run mode by default (-Apply flag needed for actual execution)
4. Sources: Personal general skills and Corp slot skills
5. Targets: ~/.kiro/skills and ~/.claude/skills
6. Also copies steering files (Kiro only)

This is a discovery/learning action - the user is examining how the sync mechanism actually works. This helps understand the architecture.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Understanding sync.ps1 junction-based skill projection mechanism</title>
  <subtitle>Examined sync script that projects canonical skill repos to CLI directories via Windows junctions</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>sync.ps1 scans two canonical sources: G:\AI\AI-canonical\skills\general and G:\AI\AI-canonical-corp\skills\slot</fact>
    <fact>Script creates Windows junctions in ~/.kiro/skills and ~/.claude/skills pointing to canonical skill sources</fact>
    <fact>Script defaults to dry-run mode; requires -Apply flag for actual execution to prevent accidental changes</fact>
    <fact>Junction removal uses rmdir to delete only the link, preserving canonical source content</fact>
    <fact>Script is idempotent; re-running rebuilds junctions without data loss</fact>
    <fact>Script also copies steering markdown files from canonical to ~/.kiro/steering directory</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The sync.ps1 script implements the v4 architecture's projection layer by scanning two canonical skill repositories (personal general skills and corporate slot skills), then creating Windows junctions in both ~/.kiro/skills and ~/.claude/skills that point back to the canonical sources. This design ensures skills are authored once in canonical locations and automatically available to both CLI tools. The script includes safety mechanisms: dry-run by default, careful junction vs real directory detection, and idempotent operation. It handles potential skill name conflicts and supports cleanup of existing junctions before recreation. Additionally handles steering file synchronization for Kiro by copying markdown files from canonical steering directory.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>G:\AI\AI-canonical\tools\sync.ps1</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
